# Financial Deep Research: Fair Isaac Corporation (FICO)

Research conducted: 2026-05-21
Stage 1 baseline provided: No — §9 notes the absence and is deferred.

---

## 1. Recent Financial Performance & Quarterly Trends

### Most Recent Report
FICO reported second quarter fiscal 2026 results on April 28, 2026. Revenue was $691.7 million, up 39% year-over-year from $498.7 million in Q2 FY2025. GAAP net income rose 63% to $264.5 million, and GAAP diluted EPS was $11.14, up 69% year-over-year. Non-GAAP diluted EPS was $12.50, up 60%. Operating income was $402.5 million, implying a GAAP operating margin of approximately 58%, while non-GAAP operating margin reached 65%, up 712 basis points year-over-year. Free cash flow was $214.3 million, and net cash provided by operating activities was $223.4 million. [Source: Company Q2 FY2026 earnings release / Title: Q2 FY2026 Earnings Report / Date: 2026-04-28] *** [Source: MarketBeat / Title: Fair Isaac Q2 Earnings Call Highlights / Date: 2026-04-28]

As of March 31, 2026, the company held $272 million in cash and marketable investments against total debt of $3.64 billion at a weighted-average interest rate of 5.5%. [Source: MarketBeat / Title: Fair Isaac Q2 Earnings Call Highlights / Date: 2026-04-28]

### Most Recent Full Year Report
For fiscal year 2025 (ended September 30, 2025), FICO reported revenue of $1.991 billion, up 16% from $1.718 billion in FY2024. Full-year GAAP net income was $652 million, equating to GAAP EPS of $26.54, up 27% and 30%, respectively. Free cash flow for the full year was $739 million, an increase of 22% year-over-year. [Source: Yahoo Finance / Title: Fair Isaac Corp Q4 2025 Earnings Call Highlights / Date: 2025-11-05] * [Source: Seeking Alpha / Title: Fair Isaac Corporation Q4 2025 Earnings Call Transcript / Date: 2025-11-05]

### Key Business Drivers in Recent Quarters
The acceleration in Q2 FY2026 was driven overwhelmingly by the Scores segment. B2B Scores revenue surged 72% year-over-year, with mortgage origination revenue up 127%, reflecting both higher unit prices and increased mortgage origination volumes. Auto originations grew 13%, while credit card and personal loan originations rose 6%. B2C Scores increased 5%, supported by indirect channel partners. [Source: TradingView / Title: FICO reports Q2 fiscal 2026 revenue $691.7M / Date: 2026-04-28] [Source: The Globe and Mail / Title: Fair Isaac Q2 2026 Earnings Call Transcript / Date: 2026-04-29]

Software segment revenue grew 7% in Q2 FY2026, with platform revenue up 54% offsetting a 12% decline in non-platform revenue. Total Software ARR reached $789 million, up 10% year-over-year, with platform ARR at $349 million (+49%) representing 44% of total Software ARR. [Source: The Globe and Mail / Title: Fair Isaac Q2 2026 Earnings Call Transcript / Date: 2026-04-29]

### Segment Performance
Scores has become increasingly dominant, contributing 68.7% of total revenue in Q2 FY2026 ($475.0 million) versus 59.6% in Q2 FY2025. Software contributed the remaining 31.3% ($216.7 million). Within Software, the platform transition is stark: platform dollar-based net retention rate was 136% while non-platform was 90%, indicating contraction in the legacy installed base. [Source: TradingView / Title: FICO reports Q2 fiscal 2026 revenue $691.7M / Date: 2026-04-28]

### Geographic & FX Exposure
Revenue is heavily concentrated in the Americas. In Q1 FY2026, management noted the regional revenue mix as 88% Americas, 8% EMEA, and 4% Asia Pacific. [Source: The Motley Fool / Title: Fair Isaac Q1 2026 Earnings Call Transcript / Date: 2026-01-28] This concentration appears consistent with prior periods; Alpha Spread data indicates approximately 87% Americas, 8% EMEA, and 5% Asia Pacific on an annual basis. [Source: Alpha Spread / Title: FICO Revenue Breakdown / Date: unknown] FX exposure is therefore limited, with the vast majority of revenue denominated in U.S. dollars.

---

## 2. Growth Trajectory

### Trajectory Assessment
A two-year look-back is appropriate but must be interpreted cautiously because FICO's growth has been non-linear, driven by discrete pricing actions and mortgage market cyclicality.

- FY2024: Revenue $1.718 billion (+16% year-over-year)
- FY2025: Revenue $1.991 billion (+16% year-over-year)
- FY2026 guidance (raised April 2026): Revenue $2.45 billion (+23% year-over-year)

The step-up from 16% to guided 23% growth in FY2026 reflects a confluence of mortgage origination recovery and continued aggressive pricing in B2B Scores. However, this rate may not be sustainable beyond FY2026. Management's commentary on the Q2 FY2026 earnings call noted that volume forecasts are conservatively estimated and that guidance "does not rely on volume increases," implying the growth is primarily price-driven. [Source: Quartr / Title: Fair Isaac Corporation Investor Relations Material / Date: 2026-05-21] * [Source: MarketBeat / Title: Fair Isaac Q2 Earnings Call Highlights / Date: 2026-04-28]

Scores segment growth has accelerated from 23% in Q1 FY2025 to 60% in Q2 FY2026, while Software growth has remained muted (2-7% in recent quarters). The company's growth algorithm is therefore increasingly dependent on the Scores segment, which carries higher regulatory and competitive risk.

### Peer-Relative Growth Context
Relative to the major credit bureaus (Equifax, Experian, TransUnion), FICO's revenue growth is comparable but its earnings growth is superior due to operating leverage and share count reduction. However, the credit bureaus are more diversified geographically and by product line, whereas FICO's recent growth is concentrated in U.S. mortgage pricing.

---

## 3. Margin & Profitability Profile

For FICO, gross margin is less analytically meaningful than operating margin because the company operates an asset-light, IP-heavy model with minimal cost of goods sold. The relevant profitability lenses are operating margin, EBITDA margin, and net margin, which capture the company's pricing power and cost discipline.

| Period | Revenue | Operating Income | GAAP Operating Margin | Non-GAAP Operating Margin |
|--------|---------|------------------|----------------------|---------------------------|
| Q2 FY2026 | $691.7M | $402.5M | 58.2% | 65.0% |
| Q1 FY2026 | $512.0M | ~$278M implied | ~54% | 54.0% |
| FY2025 (full year) | $1,991M | ~$734M | ~36.8% | Not stated |
| Q4 FY2025 | $515.8M | $197.2M | 38.2% | Not stated |

Sources: [Source: Company Q2 FY2026 earnings release / Date: 2026-04-28] *** [Source: The Motley Fool / Title: Fair Isaac Q1 2026 Earnings Call Transcript / Date: 2026-01-28] [Source: Yahoo Finance / Title: Fair Isaac Corp Q4 2025 Earnings Call Highlights / Date: 2025-11-05] [Source: SEC.gov / Title: Q4 2024 8-K Exhibit 99.1 / Date: 2024-11-06] ***

The margin expansion in Q2 FY2026 reflects operating leverage in the Scores segment—revenue grew 60% while operating expenses rose only modestly. Management indicated operating expenses were $289 million in Q2, up 4% sequentially, and expect to trend "modestly upward" in the back half of FY2026 due to personnel and marketing costs. [Source: MarketBeat / Title: Fair Isaac Q2 Earnings Call Highlights / Date: 2026-04-28]

### Peer-Relative Profitability Context
FICO's operating margin of approximately 50-65% is exceptional relative to both enterprise software and consumer data peers. KoalaGains analysis notes that FICO's operating margin "dwarfs Equifax's ~20%" and is "nearly double Experian's ~25-28%." [Source: KoalaGains / Title: Fair Isaac Corporation Competitive Analysis / Date: 2025-10-29] This margin differential is structural: FICO's Scores business requires minimal incremental capital to deliver additional units, and its IP-based model enjoys high incremental returns.

---

## 4. Cash Generation & Quality

### Cash Generation Quality Assessment
Free cash flow is the most meaningful cash metric for FICO because the business is capital-light and FCF closely approximates the economic cash available for deployment after minimal maintenance needs.

| Period | Operating Cash Flow | Capex | Free Cash Flow |
|--------|---------------------|-------|----------------|
| Q2 FY2026 | $223.4M | Minimal | $214.3M |
| Q1 FY2026 | $174.0M | ~$9M implied | $165.3M |
| FY2025 (full year) | Not explicitly stated | Minimal | $739M |
| TTM through Q2 FY2026 | Not explicitly stated | Minimal | $867M |

Sources: [Source: TradingView / Title: FICO reports Q2 fiscal 2026 revenue $691.7M / Date: 2026-04-28] [Source: Zacks / Title: Fair Isaac Q1 Earnings Top Estimates / Date: 2026-01-29] [Source: Yahoo Finance / Title: Fair Isaac Corp Q4 2025 Earnings Call Highlights / Date: 2025-11-05] [Source: The Globe and Mail / Title: Fair Isaac Q2 2026 Earnings Call Transcript / Date: 2026-04-29]

FCF conversion is strong. The company defines free cash flow as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, which consist primarily of capitalized internal-use software and minor property/equipment purchases. For the nine months ended June 30, 2025, capital expenditures totaled $26.6 million against net cash provided by operating activities of $555.1 million, yielding FCF of $528.6 million. [Source: SEC.gov / Title: Q3 2025 8-K Exhibit 99.1 / Date: 2025-07-30] ***

### Working Capital & Cash Conversion
Working capital dynamics are not material to FICO's financial analysis. The company has minimal inventory, collects receivables from large financial institutions with strong credit profiles, and carries deferred revenue on its balance sheet. Changes in operating assets and liabilities are modest relative to operating cash flow. The cash conversion cycle is not a binding constraint on the business model.

### Earnings Quality
Earnings quality is generally high but requires scrutiny of three items:
1. **Share-based compensation:** FICO excludes SBC from non-GAAP metrics. In FY2025 guidance, SBC was expected to be $157 million, or approximately 6.3% of guided revenue. [Source: SEC.gov / Title: Q4 2024 8-K Exhibit 99.1 / Date: 2024-11-06] *** This is a material non-cash expense that understates true economic costs if not accounted for.
2. **Excess tax benefit:** The company records material excess tax benefits from stock compensation ($30 million in FY2025 guidance), which are excluded from non-GAAP results but represent real cash flow benefits.
3. **Restructuring charges:** Q4 FY2025 included a pre-tax charge of $10.9 million ($0.34 per share after tax) for restructuring. [Source: Last10k / Title: Fair Isaac Corp 10-K Annual Reports / Date: 2025-11-07] *

---

## 5. Balance Sheet & Liquidity

As of March 31, 2026, FICO's balance sheet is characterized by high leverage and negative book equity, a direct consequence of aggressive share repurchases funded by debt.

| Item | Amount |
|------|--------|
| Cash & marketable investments | $272 million |
| Total debt | $3.64 billion |
| Total assets | ~$2.05 billion |
| Total liabilities | ~$4.15 billion |
| Shareholder equity | ~$(2.1) billion |
| Current ratio | 2.22 |
| Interest coverage (EBIT) | 7.2x |

Sources: [Source: MarketBeat / Title: Fair Isaac Q2 Earnings Call Highlights / Date: 2026-04-28] [Source: Simply Wall St / Title: Fair Isaac Balance Sheet & Financial Health Metrics / Date: 2026-04-15] [Source: Yahoo Finance / Title: FICO Key Statistics / Date: 2026-04-28] **

The negative equity position (~$2.1 billion) is structural, not cyclical. It results from cumulative share repurchases exceeding retained earnings. While this is not an immediate solvency risk given strong operating cash flows, it limits financial flexibility and could become problematic if earnings or cash flows decline meaningfully.

### Debt Maturity Profile & Refinancing Risk
In March 2026, FICO completed a $1.0 billion private offering of 6.250% senior unsecured notes due 2034. The proceeds were used to repay borrowings under its credit agreement, fully redeem $400 million of 5.25% senior notes due 2026, cover related fees, and support general corporate purposes including potential share repurchases. [Source: The Globe and Mail / Title: Fair Isaac Completes $1 Billion Senior Notes Offering / Date: 2026-03-21] [Source: TipRanks / Title: Fair Isaac Launches $1 Billion Senior Notes Offering / Date: 2026-03-11] *

As of Q2 FY2026, the company also carried a $265 million balance on its revolving credit facility, which is repayable at any time. The weighted-average interest rate on total debt was 5.5%. [Source: MarketBeat / Title: Fair Isaac Q2 Earnings Call Highlights / Date: 2026-04-28]

The March 2026 transaction effectively extended FICO's debt maturity profile, eliminating the near-term 2026 maturity and pushing the next significant maturity to 2034. Interest expense dollars are expected to trend "modestly upward" in the back half of FY2026 due to the higher coupon on the new notes. [Source: MarketBeat / Title: Fair Isaac Q2 Earnings Call Highlights / Date: 2026-04-28]

### Assessment
Liquidity is adequate but not robust. The company operates with minimal cash ($272 million) relative to its debt service and capex needs, relying on consistent operating cash flow generation. The interest coverage ratio of 7.2x provides a reasonable buffer, but the combination of negative equity, rising interest expense, and aggressive capital returns leaves limited room for error if revenue or margins contract.

---

## 6. Capital Deployment & Returns on Capital

### Share Repurchases
FICO's primary capital deployment mechanism is share repurchases. The company repurchased approximately $1.4 billion of stock in FY2025. In Q2 FY2026 alone, it executed the largest quarterly repurchase in company history: $605 million (484,000 shares at an average price of $1,251). In Q1 FY2026, it repurchased $163 million (95,000 shares at $1,707). [Source: The Globe and Mail / Title: Fair Isaac Q2 2026 Earnings Call Transcript / Date: 2026-04-29] [Source: The Motley Fool / Title: Fair Isaac Q1 2026 Earnings Call Transcript / Date: 2026-01-28]

In March 2026, the Board authorized a new $1.5 billion share repurchase program, following the completion of a prior $1.0 billion program. [Source: Trefis / Title: Fair Isaac Share Repurchases / Date: 2025-01-31] * Shares outstanding have declined consistently: from approximately 25.4 million in FY2022 to roughly 23.2 million as of March 2026. [Source: companiesmarketcap.com / Title: Fair Isaac Shares Outstanding / Date: unknown] [Source: Yahoo Finance / Title: FICO Key Statistics / Date: 2026-04-28] **

### Dividends
FICO does not pay a dividend. All capital return is delivered via buybacks.

### M&A / Capex
M&A activity is minimal. Capex is low and consists primarily of capitalized internal-use software costs ($21.8 million in the first nine months of FY2025) and minor property/equipment purchases. [Source: SEC.gov / Title: Q3 2025 8-K Exhibit 99.1 / Date: 2025-07-30] *** The company is not investing heavily in external growth; organic investment is focused on the FICO Platform and analytics R&D.

### Returns on Capital
FICO generates exceptionally high returns on capital. Return on assets (ttm) was 36.92% as of March 2026. [Source: Yahoo Finance / Title: FICO Key Statistics / Date: 2026-04-28] ** Third-party analysis estimates return on invested capital (ROIC) exceeding 40%, significantly above peers like Equifax (~10%), Experian (~15-18%), and TransUnion (~7-9%). [Source: KoalaGains / Title: Fair Isaac Corporation Competitive Analysis / Date: 2025-10-29]

These returns far exceed FICO's cost of capital, suggesting that every dollar retained and reinvested in the business—or used to reduce share count at reasonable valuations—creates value. However, the recent buyback pace has been so aggressive that it has been funded partly by increased debt, raising questions about whether incremental leverage is generating positive risk-adjusted returns for equity holders given the rising interest rate on new debt (6.25%).

---

## 7. Management Guidance & Forward Outlook

### Most Recent Full Year Report Guidance
For FY2025, management initially guided to revenue of $1.98 billion, GAAP net income of $624 million, GAAP EPS of $25.05, non-GAAP net income of $712 million, and non-GAAP EPS of $28.58. Actual results exceeded the revenue and EPS guidance. [Source: SEC.gov / Title: Q4 2024 8-K Exhibit 99.1 / Date: 2024-11-06] ***

### Most Recent Report Guidance
On the Q2 FY2026 earnings call (April 28, 2026), management raised full-year FY2026 guidance:
- Revenue: $2.45 billion (previously $2.35 billion at the Q1 reaffirmation)
- GAAP net income: $825 million
- GAAP EPS: $35.60 (up 34% year-over-year)
- Non-GAAP net income: $946 million
- Non-GAAP EPS: $40.45 (up 35% year-over-year)

[Source: TradingView / Title: FICO reports Q2 fiscal 2026 revenue $691.7M / Date: 2026-04-28] [Source: MarketBeat / Title: Fair Isaac Q2 Earnings Call Highlights / Date: 2026-04-28]

### Management Commentary
CEO Will Lansing expressed confidence in FY2026, stating the guidance includes "even stronger growth than we achieved in FY2025." [Source: Last10k / Title: Fair Isaac Corp 10-K Annual Reports / Date: 2025-11-07] * However, management provided two important load-bearing caveats:
1. The guidance assumes "no loss of volume to VantageScore in this fiscal year." [Source: MarketBeat / Title: Fair Isaac Q2 Earnings Call Highlights / Date: 2026-04-28]
2. The performance model (FICO Score 10T) go-live may create revenue timing shifts from late FY2026 into early FY2027 due to funding fee timing. [Source: MarketBeat / Title: Fair Isaac Q2 Earnings Call Highlights / Date: 2026-04-28]

CFO Mike Weber noted that operating expenses will trend modestly upward from the Q2 run rate, and the full-year operating tax rate is expected to be 25-26% with an effective tax rate around 24%. [Source: MarketBeat / Title: Fair Isaac Q2 Earnings Call Highlights / Date: 2026-04-28]

### Forward Claim Confidence Weighting

| Forward Claim | Confidence | Rationale |
|---------------|------------|-----------|
| FY2026 revenue of $2.45B | Medium | Q2 momentum is strong, but H2 faces tougher comps and potential VantageScore disruption |
| FY2026 GAAP EPS of $35.60 | Medium | Achievable if revenue and margins hold, but assumes continued mortgage pricing power |
| No VantageScore volume loss in FY2026 | Low-Medium | Explicitly stated as an assumption; regulatory and competitive pressure makes this uncertain |
| Software platform ARR growth >40% | Medium-High | Trend has been consistent, but off a small base; overall Software growth remains muted |
| Interest expense "modestly upward" | High | Directly observable from March 2026 refinancing |

---

## 8. Material Financial Risks & One-Off Items

1. **DOJ Antitrust Investigation:** Senator Josh Hawley (R-Mo.) publicly called on the DOJ Antitrust Division to investigate FICO in April 2025, alleging anticompetitive practices and price gouging in the credit scoring market. The letter noted FICO's roughly 90% B2B market share and price increases from $3.50 to $4.95 per mortgage score. [Source: Senator Josh Hawley / Title: Senator Hawley Calls on DOJ to Investigate FICO / Date: 2025-04-03] A March 2026 financial analysis noted that "the Department of Justice is actively investigating FICO for 'exclusionary conduct,'" though the formal status of any active investigation is not confirmed in primary filings reviewed. [Source: Finterra via FinancialContent / Title: The Gatekeeper's Dilemma: A Deep Dive into FICO / Date: 2026-03-20]

2. **VantageScore Competition and Price War:** The FHFA and FHA have moved to allow VantageScore 4.0 alongside FICO scores in the mortgage market, ending FICO's "monopoly mandate." The three major credit bureaus have reportedly begun offering VantageScore at prices as low as $0.99-$1.00 per mortgage pull, dramatically undercutting FICO's pricing. In response, FICO updated its FICO Score 10T pricing in its mortgage direct licensing program to $0.99 per score plus a $65 funding fee. [Source: Finterra via FinancialContent / Title: The Gatekeeper's Dilemma / Date: 2026-03-20] [Source: AOL / Title: Fair Isaac Q2 2026 Earnings Call Transcript / Date: 2026-04-29]

3. **Mortgage Market Cyclicality:** The Scores segment is heavily exposed to mortgage originations, which contributed 63% of total Scores revenue and 72% of B2B Scores revenue in Q2 FY2026. A downturn in mortgage volumes would disproportionately impact revenue and operating leverage. [Source: The Globe and Mail / Title: Fair Isaac Q2 2026 Earnings Call Transcript / Date: 2026-04-29]

4. **Negative Equity and Leverage:** The balance sheet carries approximately negative $2.1 billion in shareholder equity. While interest coverage remains adequate at 7.2x, the capital structure is brittle. A material decline in EBITDA could strain covenant compliance or refinancing capacity, though the recent extension of maturities to 2034 mitigates near-term refinancing risk. [Source: Simply Wall St / Title: Fair Isaac Balance Sheet & Financial Health Metrics / Date: 2026-04-15]

5. **Software Transition Risk:** The non-platform Software business is in contraction (NRR 90%, ARR declining 8%). While platform growth is strong, the overall Software segment is growing only mid-single digits. Failure to accelerate platform adoption could leave FICO overly dependent on the Scores segment.

6. **One-Off Items:** Q4 FY2025 included a $10.9 million pre-tax restructuring charge ($0.34 per share after tax). [Source: Last10k / Title: Fair Isaac Corp 10-K Annual Reports / Date: 2025-11-07] *

---

## 10. Key Financial Assumptions Embedded in Current Valuation

As of May 2026, FICO trades at approximately $1,230 per share with a market capitalization of roughly $28-29 billion (fluctuating with daily prices). [Source: Yahoo Finance / Title: FICO Historical Prices / Date: 2026-04-28] ** The forward P/E ratio based on FY2026 guidance is approximately 24.7x ($1,230 / $35.60 implied, though actual forward multiples vary with broker estimates). [Source: Yahoo Finance / Title: FICO Key Statistics / Date: 2026-04-28] **

The current valuation appears to embed the following financial assumptions:
- **Sustained pricing power:** The market assumes FICO can maintain or grow B2B Scores pricing without material volume loss to VantageScore through at least FY2027.
- **Operating leverage persistence:** Margins are assumed to remain at or above 60% (non-GAAP operating) despite competitive pressure and potential price cuts in mortgage scoring.
- **Continued aggressive buyback math:** The valuation likely assumes continued share count reduction of 3-5% annually, funded by strong FCF and manageable debt increases.
- **Software platform inflection:** The market appears to price in an eventual reacceleration of total Software growth as platform ARR offsets non-platform decline.
- **Regulatory resolution without structural remedies:** The stock price likely assumes the DOJ investigation does not result in forced unbundling, price caps, or mandated access.
- **Mortgage origination stability:** The FY2026 guidance and valuation assume mortgage volumes do not collapse.

Any deviation from these assumptions—particularly on pricing power and regulatory outcomes—would likely drive significant valuation compression given the high embedded expectations.

---

## 11. Bottom-Line Financial Assessment

### Business Model Characterization
FICO operates a hybrid business model: a quasi-monopolistic, asset-light B2B scoring franchise (dominated by U.S. mortgage and consumer credit scoring) coupled with a transitioning enterprise software division. The Scores business generates the vast majority of profit and cash flow, exhibits extreme operating leverage, and is currently in a phase of aggressive price-driven growth. The Software business is migrating from legacy on-premise solutions to a cloud-native platform, with strong platform metrics (136% NRR, 49% ARR growth) offset by a shrinking non-platform installed base. Capital is deployed almost exclusively into share repurchases, funded by organic cash generation and incremental debt, resulting in a deeply negative equity position.

### Strengths (confidence weighted)

- **Exceptional unit economics and operating leverage in Scores** (High): Incremental scoring revenue flows through at very high margins, as evidenced by 712 bps of year-over-year margin expansion in Q2 FY2026. [Source: MarketBeat / Title: Fair Isaac Q2 Earnings Call Highlights / Date: 2026-04-28]
- **Robust free cash flow generation** (High): FCF of $739 million in FY2025 and $867 million TTM through Q2 FY2026 demonstrates strong cash conversion. [Source: Yahoo Finance / Title: Fair Isaac Corp Q4 2025 Earnings Call Highlights / Date: 2025-11-05] [Source: The Globe and Mail / Title: Fair Isaac Q2 2026 Earnings Call Transcript / Date: 2026-04-29]
- **Superior returns on capital** (High): ROA of 36.9% and estimated ROIC >40% are among the highest in the technology and data sectors. [Source: Yahoo Finance / Title: FICO Key Statistics / Date: 2026-04-28] ** [Source: KoalaGains / Title: Fair Isaac Corporation Competitive Analysis / Date: 2025-10-29]
- **Platform software momentum** (Medium-High): Platform ARR growth of 49% and 136% NRR indicate product-market fit in the strategic Software segment. [Source: TradingView / Title: FICO reports Q2 fiscal 2026 revenue $691.7M / Date: 2026-04-28]
- **Disciplined cost management** (Medium): Operating expenses grew only 4% sequentially in Q2 FY2026 despite 39% revenue growth. [Source: MarketBeat / Title: Fair Isaac Q2 Earnings Call Highlights / Date: 2026-04-28]

### Weaknesses (confidence weighted)

- **Negative book equity and high financial leverage** (High): Shareholder equity of approximately negative $2.1 billion with $3.64 billion in total debt creates a brittle capital structure. [Source: Simply Wall St / Title: Fair Isaac Balance Sheet & Financial Health Metrics / Date: 2026-04-15] [Source: Yahoo Finance / Title: FICO Key Statistics / Date: 2026-04-28] **
- **Regulatory and antitrust overhang** (High): Active political and potentially DOJ scrutiny of pricing practices creates tail risk that is difficult to quantify. [Source: Senator Josh Hawley / Title: Senator Hawley Calls on DOJ to Investigate FICO / Date: 2025-04-03] [Source: Finterra via FinancialContent / Title: The Gatekeeper's Dilemma / Date: 2026-03-20]
- **Increasing dependence on mortgage origination pricing** (High): Scores revenue is 68.7% of total and mortgage originations are 63% of Scores revenue. This concentration amplifies cyclical and competitive risk. [Source: The Globe and Mail / Title: Fair Isaac Q2 2026 Earnings Call Transcript / Date: 2026-04-29]
- **VantageScore competitive threat** (Medium-High): The end of the FICO monopoly mandate at Fannie Mae/Freddie Mac and the VantageScore price war represent the first credible competitive threat in decades. [Source: Finterra via FinancialContent / Title: The Gatekeeper's Dilemma / Date: 2026-03-20]
- **Software segment transition drag** (Medium): Non-platform Software is contracting (90% NRR, -8% ARR), and total Software growth remains in the mid-single digits despite strong platform metrics. [Source: TradingView / Title: FICO reports Q2 fiscal 2026 revenue $691.7M / Date: 2026-04-28]
- **Aggressive buyback funding via debt** (Medium): The $1.5 billion buyback authorization and recent $1.0 billion debt issuance suggest buybacks are increasingly debt-financed rather than self-funded. [Source: TipRanks / Title: Fair Isaac Launches $1 Billion Senior Notes Offering / Date: 2026-03-11] *

### Watch Items
1. **DOJ investigation formal status:** Any announcement of a formal probe, subpoena, or civil investigative demand would be a material catalyst.
2. **VantageScore mortgage adoption rates:** Monitor FHFA/FHA implementation data and lender announcements regarding score model choice.
3. **FICO Score 10T pricing and funding fee economics:** The shift to $0.99 per score plus $65 funding fee needs to be monitored for net revenue impact and customer uptake.
4. **Mortgage origination volumes:** Track MBA mortgage applications as a leading indicator of Scores segment volume.
5. **Software platform vs. non-platform ARR crossover:** The point at which platform growth fully offsets non-platform decline will signal Software segment inflection.
6. **Interest coverage trend:** With higher-coupon debt (6.25%) now on the balance sheet, monitor interest coverage as a gauge of leverage sustainability.

---

## 12. Data Gaps & Limitations

1. **Stage 1 Structured Data Baseline:** Not provided at runtime; no reconciliation possible.

2. **Detailed Debt Maturity Schedule:** While the 2026 and 2034 senior notes are documented, the full maturity ladder (including revolver terms, any other senior note tranches, and exact coupon schedules) could not be fully verified from the sources searched. Search scope: SEC 10-K/10-Q filings, earnings call transcripts, TipRanks, Globe and Mail. Analytical impact: Limits precision on refinancing risk assessment beyond the known 2034 maturity.

3. **Exact FY2025 Full-Year 10-K Cash Flow Statement:** FY2025 full-year free cash flow of $739 million was cited from earnings call highlights but was not independently verified from the filed 10-K cash flow statement in the search results. Search scope: SEC EDGAR, Yahoo Finance, TradingView. Analytical impact: Minor; the figure is consistent with quarterly reported data but lacks primary filing confirmation.

4. **Formal DOJ Investigation Status:** While Senator Hawley's letter and third-party analysis claim DOJ activity, no official DOJ press release or FICO SEC filing confirming an active investigation was located. Search scope: Senator Hawley website, SEC filings, financial news. Analytical impact: Moderate; the regulatory risk is real and material, but the exact stage of any government inquiry is unverified.

5. **FX Impact Quantification:** No specific FX headwind or tailwind figures were found for the 8-13% of revenue generated outside the Americas. Search scope: Company earnings calls, 10-K geographic footnotes. Analytical impact: Low given revenue concentration, but limits precision on geographic profitability.

6. **Return on Invested Capital (ROIC) Calculation:** The >40% ROIC figure is sourced from third-party competitive analysis rather than a company-reported or independently calculated metric. Search scope: Company filings, Yahoo Finance, Trefis. Analytical impact: Moderate; the directional conclusion (very high returns) is robust, but the exact figure should be treated as an estimate.

7. **Capital Expenditure Breakdown:** Detailed capex split between maintenance and growth capital was not available. Search scope: 10-K, 10-Q cash flow statements, earnings calls. Analytical impact: Low for FCF calculation but relevant for assessing reinvestment needs.

8. **Insider Ownership and Compensation Details:** Detailed proxy data on executive compensation, option exercises, and insider transactions beyond high-level share statistics were not reviewed. Search scope: Proxy filings, Yahoo Finance insider tab. Analytical impact: Moderate; the excess tax benefit from stock compensation is material to earnings quality.

# News & Narrative Deep Research: Fair Isaac Corp (FICO)

Research conducted: 2026-05-21

## 1. Most Significant News in the Window

**FHFA Credit-Score Modernization and FICO’s Defensive Pricing Pivot.** The dominant market-moving narrative over the past 90 days—and indeed stretching back to 2025—is the disruption of FICO’s mortgage-scoring monopoly. On April 22, 2026, the Federal Housing Finance Agency (FHFA) announced an interim phase allowing Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac lenders to choose between Classic FICO and VantageScore 4.0, with full FICO Score 10T implementation to follow. [Source: FHFA / Title: Credit Scores / Date: 2026-04-22]*** VantageScore’s backers (Experian, Equifax, TransUnion) had already slashed VantageScore 4.0 pricing to as low as $0.99 per score, undercutting FICO’s previous mortgage pricing by orders of magnitude. [Source: Experian press release / Title: Experian Announces $0.99 Pricing for VantageScore 4.0 / Date: 2026-03-09]***

FICO responded during its Q2 FY2026 earnings call on April 29 by drastically restructuring its own FICO Score 10T pricing under the Mortgage Direct Licensing Program, cutting the per-score royalty from $4.95 to **$0.99** and replacing the $33 funding fee with a **$65 funding fee** per funded loan. [Source: Company Q2 2026 earnings call / Title: Q2 FY2026 Earnings Call Transcript / Date: 2026-04-29]*** CEO Will Lansing explained the shift as a deliberate move to “encourage wide use of Ten T” and to align monetization “over more players across the chain.” The market read this as a forced defensive maneuver to match a suddenly credible rival, not an offensive pricing expansion.

**Q2 FY2026 Earnings Blowout and Guidance Raise.** Despite the pricing anxiety, FICO reported exceptionally strong fiscal Q2 results on April 28, 2026: revenue of **$692 million** (+39% YoY), GAAP net income of **$264 million** (+63%), and non-GAAP EPS of **$12.50** (+60%). [Source: Company Q2 2026 earnings call / Title: Q2 FY2026 Earnings Call Transcript / Date: 2026-04-29]*** Management raised full-year fiscal 2026 guidance to **$2.45 billion** in revenue (+23%) and non-GAAP EPS of **$40.45** (+35%). Notably, CFO Steve Weber stated the guidance assumes “no loss of volume to VantageScore in this fiscal year.” [Source: Company Q2 2026 earnings call / Title: Q2 FY2026 Earnings Call Transcript / Date: 2026-04-29]***

**Steve Eisman Discloses High-Profile Short Position.** On April 30, 2026, “Big Short” investor Steve Eisman revealed he is short FICO, telling CNBC the company “very arrogantly raised prices about over 500%” and “has ticked off literally everybody in the lending world.” [Source: CNBC / Title: Steve Eisman likes the market here, but is short this software stock / Date: 2026-04-30]** The disclosure amplified competitive fears and contributed to a volatile post-earnings tape.

**UltraFICO General Availability and FICO World 2026 Product Push.** At its annual user conference (May 19–22, 2026), FICO announced the general availability of the next-generation **UltraFICO Score** powered by Plaid cash-flow data, and celebrated its 70th anniversary with a “Hello, Future” campaign. [Source: Business Wire / Title: Next-Generation UltraFICO Score Now Available / Date: 2026-05-20]*** The company also showcased a **T-Mobile** platform win for customer onboarding. [Source: FICO press release / Title: T-Mobile Delivers Faster, More Reliable Customer Onboarding with FICO Platform / Date: 2026-05-19]*** Concurrently, FICO released a Milliman-commissioned study asserting FICO Score 10T is the most predictive model for first-time homebuyer mortgage risk versus VantageScore 4.0. [Source: Milliman / Title: Analysis of credit bureau data and mortgage fit statistics for FICO Score 10T and Vantage Score 4.0 / Date: 2026-05-18]*

## 2. Earnings Call Deep Dive

### Headline Takeaways
Fiscal Q2 was a record quarter by most financial metrics. Scores segment revenue surged **60% YoY** to $475 million, driven by a **127% jump in mortgage originations revenue**. B2B scores were the primary engine, while B2C scores grew for the sixth consecutive quarter (+5%). Software revenue grew 7%, including 54% platform revenue growth offset by a 12% decline in non-platform license sales. [Source: Company Q2 2026 earnings call / Title: Q2 FY2026 Earnings Call Transcript / Date: 2026-04-29]*** Non-GAAP operating margin expanded 712 basis points to **65%**. The company executed a record **$605 million** in share repurchases during the quarter.

### Management Tone & Conviction
Lansing projected confidence in FICO’s competitive position but the tone on mortgage scoring was more defensive than in prior quarters. When asked about VantageScore, Lansing did not dismiss the threat but framed it as a battle FICO would win on predictiveness: “We don't see good reasons to switch,” and noted that 10T at $0.99 is “at parity with Vantage at $0.99.” [Source: Company Q2 2026 earnings call / Title: Q2 FY2026 Earnings Call Transcript / Date: 2026-04-29]***

On the pricing-model shift, Lansing portrayed the $0.99 + $65 funding-fee structure as a long-planned strategic evolution rather than a reactive price cut: “We’ve been talking about this for a couple of years now... moving away from charging primarily upfront first score and instead distributing monetization over more players across the chain.” [Source: Company Q2 2026 earnings call / Title: Q2 FY2026 Earnings Call Transcript / Date: 2026-04-29]*** Whether investors fully bought this framing is debatable—the stock remained volatile post-call.

CFO Steve Weber was unequivocal on capital returns, calling the $605 million quarterly buyback “the single largest quarterly repurchase in dollars in FICO history,” and noted an additional $170 million in repurchases since April 1 at an average price of $1,040. [Source: Company Q2 2026 earnings call / Title: Q2 FY2026 Earnings Call Transcript / Date: 2026-04-29]***

### Q&A Pushback & Analyst Skepticism
Analysts pressed hardest on the sustainability of mortgage-score pricing and the mechanics of the Direct License Program. Wells Fargo’s Jason Haas directly challenged Lansing on the “philosophy behind adjusting your pricing model going to the $0.99 upfront,” suggesting theStreet wanted clarity on whether this was a margin sacrifice to defend share. [Source: Company Q2 2026 earnings call / Title: Q2 FY2026 Earnings Call Transcript / Date: 2026-04-29]***

Management acknowledged that the new performance model would create a revenue lag because the $65 funding fee is collected at loan funding rather than at score pull, potentially shifting some late-fiscal-2026 revenue into early fiscal 2027. Analysts also questioned how FHFA would handle “gaming” scenarios where consumers rate-shop and lenders pull multiple scores. Lansing conceded that if the system can be gamed, originators may end up pulling both FICO and VantageScore, implying some volume overlap but not necessarily a loss.

### Forward Commentary & Guidance
Fiscal 2026 guidance was raised across the board: revenue to **$2.45 billion**, GAAP EPS to **$35.60**, and non-GAAP EPS to **$40.45**. The guidance embeds two critical assumptions: (1) no volume loss to VantageScore in FY2026, and (2) modestly higher operating expenses in the back half due to FICO World and Scores marketing. [Source: Company Q2 2026 earnings call / Title: Q2 FY2026 Earnings Call Transcript / Date: 2026-04-29]*** Interest expense is expected to trend “modestly upward” following the March 2026 $1 billion senior notes issuance.

## 3. Analyst Coverage & Rating Changes

### Consensus Picture
As of May 21, 2026, the consensus rating is **Overweight/Buy**, with approximately 14 Buy/Overweight ratings, 4 Hold/Neutral ratings, and 2 Sell/Underweight ratings among roughly 20 covering firms. The average price target sits near **$1,510–$1,620**, though the dispersion is extraordinarily wide—from a low of $707 (HSBC, dated September 2023) to a high of $2,400 (Jefferies, January 2026). [Source: MarketWatch / Title: Fair Isaac Corp. Analyst Estimates / Date: 2026-05-21]*; [Source: Benzinga / Title: Fair Isaac Analyst Ratings and Price Targets / Date: 2026-05-21]* The wide spread reflects a binary debate: bulls see a temporarily dislocated quality compounder, while bears fear structural margin compression.

### Specific Rating Actions in the Window
- **Bank of America (Curtis Nagle):** Maintained Buy but cut price target to **$1,400** from $1,550 on May 19, 2026, implying analysts are factoring in competitive headwinds despite solid execution. [Source: GuruFocus / Title: FICO Maintains by B of A Securities -- Price Target Lowered to $1400 / Date: 2026-05-20]*
- **Jefferies (Surinder Thind):** Maintained Buy, price target **$1,700** (May 4, 2026). [Source: TickerNerd / Title: FICO Stock Forecast 2026 / Date: 2026-05-21]*
- **JP Morgan (Alexander Hess):** Maintained Neutral, price target **$1,225** (April 30, 2026). [Source: MarketBeat / Title: Fair Isaac Price Target Cut to $1,225.00 by Analysts at JPMorgan / Date: 2026-04-30]*
- **Wells Fargo (Jason Haas):** Maintained Overweight, price target **$1,400** (April 30, 2026). [Source: Moomoo / Title: Fair Isaac Jumps 6.4% as Upbeat Earnings Follow-through / Date: 2026-05-19]*
- **Needham:** Maintained Buy, price target **$1,650** (April 29, 2026). [Source: TickerNerd / Title: FICO Stock Forecast 2026 / Date: 2026-05-21]*

The post-earnings cluster of target cuts (BofA, earlier JP Morgan) versus maintained ratings suggests the sell side is acknowledging elevated uncertainty without abandoning the long-term bull case.

## 4. Regulatory, Legal & Compliance Developments

**FHFA Credit Score Modernization Interim Phase.** On April 22, 2026, FHFA directed Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac to permit approved lenders to deliver loans using either Classic FICO or VantageScore 4.0 on an interim basis. The Enterprises expect to publish historical FICO 10T scores in Summer 2026, with adoption to follow. [Source: FHFA / Title: Credit Scores / Date: 2026-04-22]*** This is not new policy—the validation dates to 2022—but the April 2026 operationalization crystallized the competitive threat.

**Senator Hawley Antitrust Investigation.** On March 24, 2026, Senator Josh Hawley (R-MO) announced an investigation into FICO’s mortgage pricing practices and sent letters to the DOJ Antitrust Division and FTC urging formal probes. Hawley alleged that FICO increased per-score mortgage pricing from roughly $0.60 to $10.00 over five years, calling an 88% operating margin “not hallmarks of a competitive market.” [Source: HousingWire / Title: Hawley probes FICO mortgage credit score pricing / Date: 2026-03-24]** FICO responded that its fees represent a small fraction of closing costs. As of May 21, 2026, no formal DOJ or FTC action has been publicly announced in response.

**Ongoing Political Criticism from FHFA Leadership.** FHFA Director Bill Pulte has repeatedly criticized FICO’s pricing, including a 2025 statement calling the firm a “monopoly who has ripped off Americans for decades.” [Source: CNBC / Title: FICO CEO defends credit score pricing amid FHFA criticism / Date: 2025-07-31]** While this predates the 90-day window, it remains an active narrative driver that contextualizes the April 2026 policy actions.

No material litigation or settlement developments were identified in the research window beyond the regulatory/political overhang described above.

## 5. Corporate Events: M&A, Leadership, Restructuring, Capital Actions

### M&A Activity
No M&A transactions were announced in the window.

### Leadership Changes
No material leadership changes in the window.

### Restructuring & Workforce Actions
No restructuring or workforce actions identified.

### Capital Return Announcements
**Buybacks.** In February/March 2026, FICO’s Board authorized a new **$1.5 billion** share repurchase program. In Q2 FY2026, the company repurchased **$605 million** of stock (484,000 shares at ~$1,251/share), the largest quarterly buyback in company history. Management noted an additional **$170 million** in repurchases since April 1 at ~$1,040/share, capitalizing on the depressed stock price. [Source: Company Q2 2026 earnings call / Title: Q2 FY2026 Earnings Call Transcript / Date: 2026-04-29]***; [Source: Yahoo Finance / Title: Fair Isaac Corporation Announces $1.5 Billion Share Buyback Program / Date: 2026-03-03]*

**Debt Management.** In March 2026, FICO issued **$1 billion** in 6.25% senior notes due 2034 and used proceeds to redeem **$400 million** of 5.25% notes due May 2026, reducing near-term maturity risk. Total debt stood at **$3.64 billion** at quarter end. [Source: Company Q2 2026 earnings call / Title: Q2 FY2026 Earnings Call Transcript / Date: 2026-04-29]***

The market is reading the buybacks as a dual signal: confidence in free cash flow generation, but also a substitute for organic growth reacceleration in the Software segment.

### Material Insider Transactions
Insider activity in the window appears routine. CEO William Lansing reported a stock gift transaction on May 15, 2026. CFO Steven Weber reported a sale of 310 shares at $1,098.59 on May 19, 2026. [Source: Seeking Alpha / Title: Fair Isaac Corporation (FICO) Stock Price, Quote, News & Analysis / Date: 2026-05-22]* No clustered open-market buying or selling by multiple insiders was identified. The most recent material CEO sale was a $9.2 million disposition in September 2025, outside the window. [Source: Investing.com / Title: Fair Isaac CEO Lansing sells $9.2m in stock / Date: 2025-09-11]*

## 6. Sentiment & Positioning

### Institutional Sentiment
Institutional ownership remains high at approximately **91.4%** of shares outstanding. [Source: ShortSqueeze / Title: Fair Isaac Corporation Short Interest / Date: 2026-May]* Recent 13F data is mixed and somewhat stale. Capital Research Global Investors opened a new ~$516.6 million position in Q4 2025, while T. Rowe Price and JPMorgan Chase reduced holdings in Q3 2025. [Source: Quiver Quantitative / Title: Fund Update: New $516.6M $FICO stock position opened by Capital Research Global Investors / Date: 2026-02-11]* Q1 2026 granular 13F flow data was not reliably accessible across aggregators.

### Retail & Social Sentiment
Retail sentiment is not a material driver of the FICO narrative. The stock is thinly traded (~364K average daily volume) and not a prominent social-media topic. Any retail discussion is largely derivative of institutional debates around VantageScore competition. Marked as lower-signal.

### Short Interest
Short interest sits at approximately **1.56 million shares** as of mid-May 2026, representing **~6.7–6.9% of float** with a days-to-cover ratio of roughly **3.3–4.3**. [Source: ShortSqueeze / Title: Fair Isaac Corporation Short Interest / Date: 2026-May]*; [Source: Seeking Alpha / Title: Fair Isaac Corporation (FICO) Stock Price, Quote, News & Analysis / Date: 2026-05-22]* This is elevated relative to historical norms but not at extreme squeeze levels. The short base likely includes event-driven funds betting on regulatory or share-loss catalysts.

### Valuation Narrative
The dominant valuation debate centers on whether FICO deserves a premium multiple given the existential competitive threat to its mortgage-scoring cash cow. The stock has fallen approximately **44% from its 52-week high of $2,199.92** (reached in 2025) to ~$1,228 as of May 21, 2026. [Source: Robinhood / Title: FICO Stock Price Quote & News / Date: 2026-05-21]* Bulls argue the selloff overdiscounts the moat and ignores the non-mortgage Scores and Platform businesses. Bears argue that even at ~37x forward earnings, the stock prices in a VantageScore share-loss scenario that management refuses to acknowledge in guidance.

## 7. Short-Seller Research & Activist Investor Involvement

**Steve Eisman Short Disclosure.** On April 30, 2026, Steve Eisman of Neuberger Berman (famed for “The Big Short”) publicly disclosed a short position in FICO. On CNBC, he stated: “I think FICO very arrogantly has raised prices about over 500% over the last many, many years, has ticked off literally everybody in the lending world.” He compared FICO’s effective charge per 100 mortgage applications (~$2,000) to VantageScore’s (~$99) and concluded the pricing power is unsustainable. [Source: CNBC / Title: Steve Eisman likes the market here, but is short this software stock / Date: 2026-04-30]** The stock dropped roughly 7% intraday following the disclosure. [Source: Investing.com / Title: FICO stock falls after Steve Eisman discloses short position / Date: 2026-04-30]*

No activist 13D filings or public engagement campaigns were identified in the window. No formal short-seller research reports (e.g., Hindenburg, Muddy Waters) were published on FICO in the period.

## 8. Narrative Shifts

**A clear, identifiable narrative shift has occurred.** 

- **Prior Narrative (through late 2025):** FICO was a “compounder with monopoly economics”—a capital-light, pricing-power-driven information-services business with an impenetrable moat in mortgage scoring, producing 50%+ operating margins and relentless EPS growth via buybacks.

- **Current Narrative (from early 2026):** FICO is an “embattled incumbent defending its moat” against a credible, regulator-backed competitor (VantageScore), political attacks from Congress and the FHFA, and customer revolt over years of 500%+ price hikes.

- **Trigger:** The confluence of (1) FHFA Director Pulte’s open war on FICO pricing in 2025; (2) the FHFA’s April 2026 interim approval of VantageScore 4.0 for GSE loans; (3) the credit bureaus’ $0.99 VantageScore pricing; and (4) FICO’s forced defensive price cut on FICO Score 10T to $0.99. The stock’s 44% drawdown from peak reflects this reframing, even as financial results have accelerated.

## 9. Narrative-vs-Reality Disconnects

**A material disconnect exists between the dominant bearish narrative and observable fundamentals.**

The prevailing market story assumes structural degradation of FICO’s Scores pricing power and impending share loss to VantageScore. Yet the company just reported its strongest quarter in recent memory: Scores revenue up 60%, mortgage originations revenue up 127%, non-GAAP operating margin at 65%, and management raised guidance while explicitly assuming **no volume loss to VantageScore in fiscal 2026.** [Source: Company Q2 2026 earnings call / Title: Q2 FY2026 Earnings Call Transcript / Date: 2026-04-29]*** The stock trades at a sharp discount to its 2025 peak despite 35% non-GAAP EPS growth guidance, suggesting the market is pricing in a long-term earnings collapse that has not materialized and is not projected by management or the sell-side consensus for the current fiscal year.

The bull/bear disconnect boils down to timing: bears believe FY2026 guidance is a lagging indicator and that VantageScore adoption will accelerate in FY2027; bulls believe FICO’s predictiveness advantage and entrenched workflows will preserve share, making the current valuation a dislocation.

## 10. Bottom-Line Narrative Assessment

### Narrative Characterization
FICO is currently a **battleground stock** caught between demonstrably superb near-term fundamentals and a structurally threatened core revenue stream. The market is treating the mortgage-scoring business as a melting ice cube—pricing power permanently impaired by regulatory intervention and VantageScore competition—while management insists the moat is intact and FICO Score 10T will win on merit. Capital returns (record buybacks) are providing downside support but cannot alone re-rate the stock if the Scores narrative deteriorates further.

### Key Narrative Drivers (confidence weighted)
1. **Mortgage-scoring pricing power defense** — *High confidence.* The FHFA/VantageScore threat is real, priced in, and actively reshaping FICO’s pricing behavior. This is the single most important driver of the stock.
2. **FICO Score 10T adoption timeline and GSE implementation** — *Medium confidence.* FHFA has signaled historical 10T data release in Summer 2026, but exact go-live dates for GSE adoption remain vague. Management expects “final sign-off” on reseller calculations but gave no firm date.
3. **Magnitude and sustainability of capital returns** — *High confidence.* FICO generated $867 million in trailing-four-quarter free cash flow and is deploying it aggressively ($1.5B authorization, record quarterly buybacks). This is well-evidenced and widely held.
4. **Software Platform ARR growth (33% YoY)** — *Medium confidence.* Platform momentum is strong ($315M+ ARR, 54% revenue growth), but the Software segment as a whole is only growing 7% due to legacy license declines, limiting its ability to offset Scores volatility.
5. **Regulatory/political overhang (DOJ/FTC, Congressional action)** — *Medium confidence.* Hawley’s letters and FHFA criticism are concrete. Whether they translate into formal enforcement or legislation is uncertain.

### Narrative Risks (confidence weighted)
1. **VantageScore gains material mortgage share faster than expected** — *High confidence.* The $0.99 pricing and GSE approval create a viable alternative for the first time in decades. If lenders switch en masse, FICO’s 91% Scores margin profile is at risk.
2. **Regulatory price caps or mandated licensing changes** — *Medium confidence.* Congressional or FHFA action to cap scoring fees or mandate bi-merge reporting would directly compress revenue per loan.
3. **Management guidance proves optimistic on VantageScore impact** — *Medium confidence.* The FY2026 assumption of zero volume loss could be challenged as early as Q3 or Q4 if lender adoption accelerates.
4. **Elevated leverage limiting strategic flexibility** — *Low-medium confidence.* With $3.64 billion in debt and rising interest expense, the company’s ability to maintain buyback intensity while funding growth is finite.

### Catalysts to Watch
- **Q3 FY2026 Earnings:** Expected August 5, 2026. First quarter with potential early read on VantageScore competitive impact and 10T direct-license revenue lag.
- **FHFA Final Sign-off on FICO Score 10T Reseller Calculations:** Management cited this as a remaining dependency with no firm date. Could unlock 10T volume in conforming market.
- **GSE Historical FICO 10T Data Release:** Scheduled for Summer 2026 (June–August window). Will allow lenders to model portfolio impact and could accelerate adoption decisions.
- **DOJ/FTC Response to Senator Hawley:** No formal response announced as of May 21, 2026. Any confirmation of an antitrust probe would be a negative catalyst.
- **FICO World Customer Adoption Announcements:** Ongoing May 19–22, 2026. Watch for additional platform customer wins or lender commitments to 10T/UltraFICO.

## 11. Data Gaps & Limitations

- **Granular Q1 2026 13F institutional flow data:** Aggregator sites (HedgeFollow, WhaleWisdom) displayed incomplete or empty tables for recent quarterly position changes. Search scope: HedgeFollow, WhaleWisdom, Quiver Quantitative, SEC EDGAR. Impact: Cannot precisely quantify which institutions were buyers vs. sellers in the most recent quarter.

- **DOJ/FTC official response to Senator Hawley:** No public response or confirmation/denial of an investigation was located. Search scope: HousingWire, CNBC, Reuters, DOJ press releases. Impact: Regulatory risk remains a known unknown; inability to confirm whether the Hawley letter is political noise or a precursor to formal action.

- **BofA Securities full research note rationale for May 19 target cut:** Only headline data was available. Search scope: GuruFocus, MarketBeat, Bloomberg. Impact: Cannot fully characterize the specific model assumptions (e.g., mortgage pricing decay rate) driving the $1,400 target.

- **Detailed options positioning beyond short interest:** No reliable data on institutional call/put accumulation or dark-pool flow. Search scope: Nasdaq options chain, Barchart, MarketChameleon. Impact: Sentiment section relies on short interest and analyst ratings rather than derivatives positioning.

- **Insider transaction details from May 15, 2026:** Only a summary reference to “two conversions and one tax payment” was found. Search scope: Yahoo Finance insider transactions, SEC Form 4 database. Impact: Cannot confirm whether any May 2026 insider activity was material or purely routine.
